headline: Should Allstate’s Q2 Catastrophe Hit and Steady Dividend Require Action From Allstate (ALL) Investors?
summary: In July 2026, Allstate reported estimated catastrophe losses of US$563 million for June and US$1.72 billion for the second quarter, with the after-tax impacts at US$445 million and US$1.36 billion respectively. Alongside these heavy catastrophe costs, Allstate’s board also maintained its quarterly dividend at US$1.08 per share, underscoring an ongoing capital return commitment. We’ll now examine how these elevated catastrophe losses, alongside the dividend decision, may influence Allstate’s...
